steps.title: Identify the verb form that establishes the narrative sequence
steps.content: The phrase “would become known” at the end of the sentence is the key. The “would” form here establishes how the sentence narrates the consequences of Zewail's 1987 discovery.

steps.title: Apply the rule of tense consistency in coordinate sequences
steps.content:
”Would be honored” creates the parallel “would” form that “would become” establishes, properly narrating how honors and fame both resulted from the 1987 discovery.

The other three choices—is honored, will be honored, and had been honored—break the tense consistency required in coordinated narrative sequences.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
